name: worktree
context: Work on multiple branches simultaneously using linked worktrees — no stashing or branch switching needed
info: Git worktrees let you check out multiple branches at the same time in separate directories, all linked to the same repository. Instead of stashing or committing WIP to switch branches, you create a worktree for the other branch and work in both simultaneously. Each worktree has its own working tree and index but shares the same .git object database and refs. This is especially powerful for agents that need to work on a feature while simultaneously reviewing or testing another branch. Worktrees are also ideal for running tests on one branch while developing on another. A branch can only be checked out in one worktree at a time. See [[git/branch]] for branch management, [[git/stash]] for the alternative approach of shelving changes, and [[git/config]] for worktree-specific configuration.
triggers: work on two branches at once
check out a branch in a separate directory
body:
# Git Worktree

Worktrees let you have multiple branches checked out simultaneously in different directories, all sharing the same git repository.

## Why Worktrees?

Without worktrees, working on another branch means:
1. Stash or commit your current work
2. Switch branches
3. Do the other work
4. Switch back
5. Unstash or continue

With worktrees:
1. Create a worktree for the other branch
2. Work in both directories simultaneously
3. Remove the worktree when done

No stashing, no context switching, no lost work.

## Creating Worktrees

```bash
# Create a worktree for an existing branch
git worktree add ../hotfix-dir hotfix-branch

# Create a worktree with a new branch
git worktree add -b new-feature ../feature-dir main

# Create a worktree at a specific path
git worktree add /tmp/review-branch review-branch

# Create a detached HEAD worktree (for a tag or commit)
git worktree add --detach ../release-check v2.0
```

The first argument is the directory path, the second is the branch to check out. The directory is created automatically.

## Listing Worktrees

```bash
# List all worktrees
git worktree list

# Verbose listing with branch info
git worktree list --porcelain
```

Output:
```
/home/user/project          abc1234 [main]
/home/user/project-hotfix   def5678 [hotfix-login]
```

## Working in a Worktree

Each worktree is a normal directory. All git commands work as expected:

```bash
cd ../hotfix-dir
git status
git add .
git commit -m "fix: resolve login issue"
git push origin hotfix-branch
```

The worktree shares the same object database, so:
- Commits made in any worktree are visible from all worktrees
- Branches, tags, and refs are shared
- Fetches from any worktree update all worktrees

## Removing Worktrees

```bash
# Remove a worktree (after you're done)
git worktree remove ../hotfix-dir

# Force remove (even if dirty)
git worktree remove --force ../hotfix-dir

# Clean up stale worktree references
git worktree prune
```

## Constraints

- **One branch per worktree**: A branch can only be checked out in one worktree at a time. Attempting to check it out in a second worktree fails.
- **No nested worktrees**: You can't create a worktree inside another worktree.
- **Shared refs**: Changes to branches/tags in one worktree are immediately visible in others.

```bash
# If you need the same branch in two places, create a new branch:
git worktree add -b feature-copy ../copy-dir feature-branch
```

## Common Workflows

### Hotfix While Developing

```bash
# You're on feature-branch, urgent fix needed
git worktree add ../hotfix hotfix-urgent main
cd ../hotfix
# Fix the bug
git commit -am "fix: urgent production issue"
git push origin hotfix-urgent
cd ../project
git worktree remove ../hotfix
```

### Code Review

```bash
# Review a PR branch without leaving your current work
git fetch origin
git worktree add ../review-pr origin/pr-branch
cd ../review-pr
# Review, test, run linters
cd ../project
git worktree remove ../review-pr
```

### Running Tests on Another Branch

```bash
# Run test suite on main while developing on feature
git worktree add ../test-main main
cd ../test-main && npm test
cd ../project  # continue working
git worktree remove ../test-main
```

## Agent Best Practices

1. **Use worktrees instead of stash** — more explicit, less error-prone
2. **Place worktrees in sibling directories** — `../name` keeps things organized
3. **Remove when done** — don't leave worktrees lingering
4. **Name directories descriptively** — `../hotfix-login` not `../tmp`
5. **Prune regularly** — `git worktree prune` cleans up manually deleted directories
6. **Combine with agents** — spawn a sub-agent in a worktree for parallel work

sections.title: Creating worktrees
sections.body: `git worktree add <path> <branch>` creates a new working directory with that branch checked out. Add `-b <new-branch> <start-point>` to create a new branch. Add `--detach` for a detached HEAD. The directory is created automatically and linked to the main repository. Each worktree gets its own index (staging area) and working tree but shares .git/objects and .git/refs.

sections.title: Listing and removing worktrees
sections.body: `git worktree list` shows all worktrees with paths, HEAD commits, and branches. `git worktree remove <path>` cleans up a worktree (fails if dirty — use `--force` to override). `git worktree prune` removes references to worktrees whose directories were deleted manually. Always remove worktrees you're done with to keep the list clean.

sections.title: Common worktree workflows
sections.body: **Hotfix**: Create a worktree from main, fix the bug, push, remove the worktree. **Code review**: Fetch the PR branch, create a worktree, review/test, remove. **Parallel testing**: Create a worktree for main, run tests there while developing on feature. **Agent parallelism**: Spawn sub-agents in separate worktrees for independent tasks. Worktrees avoid the stash-switch-unstash dance entirely.

sections.title: Worktree constraints
sections.body: Each branch can only be checked out in one worktree at a time. Worktrees can't be nested inside each other. All worktrees share the same object database and refs — a commit in one is visible in all. Lock files (.git/worktrees/<name>/locked) prevent accidental removal. Use `git worktree lock` and `unlock` for worktrees on removable media.

links.context: Branches are checked out in worktrees
Worktrees are an alternative to stashing

related.note: Worktrees eliminate the need to stash when switching contexts
Branches must exist (or be created) to check out in worktrees

scripts.name: Worktree Manager
scripts.description: Create, list, and remove worktrees with conventions
scripts.inline:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ACTION="${1:?Usage: worktree-manager.sh <create|list|remove|cleanup> [args...]}"
shift

# Worktrees go in sibling directory by convention
REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
PARENT=$(dirname "$REPO_ROOT")
REPO_NAME=$(basename "$REPO_ROOT")

case "$ACTION" in
  create)
    BRANCH="${1:?Branch name required}"
    BASE="${2:-HEAD}"
    WTPATH="$PARENT/${REPO_NAME}-wt-${BRANCH//\//-}"
    if [ -d "$WTPATH" ]; then
      echo "Worktree already exists at $WTPATH"
      exit 1
    fi
    # Check if branch exists
    if git rev-parse --verify "$BRANCH" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      git worktree add "$WTPATH" "$BRANCH"
    else
      echo "Creating new branch $BRANCH from $BASE"
      git worktree add -b "$BRANCH" "$WTPATH" "$BASE"
    fi
    echo "Worktree created at: $WTPATH"
    echo "cd $WTPATH"
    ;;
  list)
    git worktree list
    ;;
  remove)
    BRANCH="${1:?Branch name required}"
    WTPATH="$PARENT/${REPO_NAME}-wt-${BRANCH//\//-}"
    if [ ! -d "$WTPATH" ]; then
      echo "No worktree at $WTPATH"
      echo "Current worktrees:"
      git worktree list
      exit 1
    fi
    git worktree remove "$WTPATH"
    echo "Removed worktree: $WTPATH"
    ;;
  cleanup)
    echo "Pruning stale worktree references..."
    git worktree prune -v
    echo
    echo "Current worktrees:"
    git worktree list
    ;;
  *)
    echo "Unknown action: $ACTION"
    echo "Usage: worktree-manager.sh <create|list|remove|cleanup> [args...]"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

branch.description: Branch to check out in the worktree

base.description: Base ref for new branches (default: HEAD)

path.description: Custom path for the worktree directory

author: dojo-community
